items.title: Middle East war live: Iran vows response after US destroyer fires on cargo ship
items.standfirst: Iran vowed retaliation after a US destroyer fired on an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman on Sunday, as it was attempting to evade a US naval blockade. Tehran condemned the incident as “armed piracy” and warned its forces would respond soon. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: Iran vowed retaliation after a US destroyer fired on an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman on Sunday, as it was attempting to evade a US naval blockade. Tehran condemned the incident as “armed piracy” and warned its forces would respond soon. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: US forces attack and seize Iranian ship Touska near Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: US Central Command has published a video said to show a guided-missile destroyer firing at an Iranian-flagged ship.
items.content: US Central Command has published a video said to show a guided-missile destroyer firing at an Iranian-flagged ship.

items.title: US intercepts and seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship, Trump says
items.standfirst: Tehran has not commented on the incident, which comes as the US prepares for a second round of talks.
items.content: Tehran has not commented on the incident, which comes as the US prepares for a second round of talks.

items.title: US military seized Iranian-flagged container ship, Trump says
items.standfirst: Trump says cargo ship tried to get past US naval blockade near strait of Hormuz ‘and it did not go well for them’The US military took custody of an Iranian-flagged container ship that attempted to get past an American blockade near the strait of Hormuz, Donald Trump announced on Sunday.In a social media post, Trump said that an “Iranian-flagged cargo ship named TOUSKA” tried to get past the US naval blockade, “and it did not go well for them”. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Tehran has ‘no plans to participate’ in talks with US, state media reports
items.standfirst: Reported response comes hours after Trump announced delegation to Islamabad, having earlier threatened to raze Iran’s infrastructureMiddle East crisis – live updatesTehran is not currently planning to take part in new talks with the US, Iran state media reported on Sunday evening, hours after Donald Trump said he was dispatching negotiators to Islamabad.“There are currently no plans to participate in the next round of Iran-US talks,” state broadcaster IRIB said, citing Iranian sources. Continue reading...
items.content: Reported response comes hours after Trump announced delegation to Islamabad, having earlier threatened to raze Iran’s infrastructureMiddle East crisis – live updatesTehran is not currently planning to take part in new talks with the US, Iran state media reported on Sunday evening, hours after Donald Trump said he was dispatching negotiators to Islamabad.“There are currently no plans to participate in the next round of Iran-US talks,” state broadcaster IRIB said, citing Iranian sources. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump says US delegation will travel to Islamabad on Monday, Iran declines talks
items.standfirst: Iran is not currently planning to attend the next round of talks with the US, which has announced that it would send a team of negotiators to Pakistan on Monday, Iranian state television reported on Sunday.
items.content: Iran is not currently planning to attend the next round of talks with the US, which has announced that it would send a team of negotiators to Pakistan on Monday, Iranian state television reported on Sunday.

items.title: Trump says US seized Iranian ship trying to get past blockade near Hormuz
items.standfirst: Iran vows retaliation, accuses US of violating ceasefire after attack on cargo ship Touska.
items.content: Iran vows retaliation, accuses US of violating ceasefire after attack on cargo ship Touska.

items.title: Trump sending US delegation for Iran talks in Pakistan
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump said US negotiators will head to Pakistan on Monday for another round of talks with Iran, raising hopes of extending a fragile ceasefire set to expire by Wednesday even as Washington and Tehran remain in a standoff over the Strait of Hormuz. There ⁠is ​currently ​no decision by Iran to ​send ‌a ⁠negotiating ‌delegation to ⁠Pakistan "as long as there ​is ‌a naval blockade," ‌Iran's ​Tasnim news agency reported on Sunday. Douglas Herbert, FRANCE 24's international affairs commentator tells us more.
items.content: US President Donald Trump said US negotiators will head to Pakistan on Monday for another round of talks with Iran, raising hopes of extending a fragile ceasefire set to expire by Wednesday even as Washington and Tehran remain in a standoff over the Strait of Hormuz. There ⁠is ​currently ​no decision by Iran to ​send ‌a ⁠negotiating ‌delegation to ⁠Pakistan "as long as there ​is ‌a naval blockade," ‌Iran's ​Tasnim news agency reported on Sunday. Douglas Herbert, FRANCE 24's international affairs commentator tells us more.

items.title: What to know about US-Iran standoff over the Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: Trump accuses Iran of violating the ceasefire as he announces plans to send US negotiators to Pakistan for more talks.
items.content: Trump accuses Iran of violating the ceasefire as he announces plans to send US negotiators to Pakistan for more talks.

items.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz

items.content: The U.S. has taken custody of an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz after firing on its engine room, President Trump said in a post on social media. (Image credit: Asghar Besharati)

bullet_summary: Eight children and teens were killed in a mass shooting in Shreveport, Louisiana, on Sunday, police said
The victims ranged in age from 1 to about 14 years old, said Shreveport, La
The victims' ages ranged from one to about 14 years old, said Shreveport police spokesperson Chris Bordelon

items.title: Mass shooting rampage in Louisiana leaves eight children dead and others wounded
items.standfirst: Shreveport police say suspect Shamar Elkins, who was fatally shot, killed seven of his children and injured their mother in a ‘domestic violence incident’Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxAt least eight children were killed, and two adults were wounded in a mass shooting in the Louisiana city of Shreveport, in what police called a “domestic violence incident”.Chris Bordelon, the Shreveport police department spokesperson, said on Sunday evening that the suspect, Shamar Elkins, killed seven of his own children and wounded their mother, as well as killing another child. Continue reading...

items.title: Eight children killed in Louisiana shooting, police say
items.standfirst: The children, aged from one to 14 years old, were killed in a shooting that police are describing as a "domestic disturbance".
items.content: The children, aged from one to 14 years old, were killed in a shooting that police are describing as a "domestic disturbance".

items.title: 8 children killed in mass shooting in Shreveport, Louisiana, police say
items.standfirst: Eight children and teens were killed early Sunday in a mass shooting in Shreveport, Louisiana. The suspected shooter is also dead.
items.content: Eight children and teens were killed early Sunday in a mass shooting in Shreveport, Louisiana. The suspected shooter is also dead.

items.title: Louisiana officials speak after 8 children and teens killed in shooting
items.standfirst: Eight children and teens were killed in a mass shooting in Shreveport, Louisiana, on Sunday, police said. The incident was described as "domestic in nature" by authorities.
items.content: Eight children and teens were killed in a mass shooting in Shreveport, Louisiana, on Sunday, police said. The incident was described as "domestic in nature" by authorities.

items.title: Police give early update on Shreveport, Louisiana mass shooting
items.standfirst: Officials in Louisiana held a news conference after 10 people were shot in a domestic disturbance that left eight children dead. Corporal Chris Bordelon of the Shreveport Police Department, Mayor Tom Arceneaux and Chief Wayne Smith provided updates on Sunday afternoon.
items.content: Officials in Louisiana held a news conference after 10 people were shot in a domestic disturbance that left eight children dead. Corporal Chris Bordelon of the Shreveport Police Department, Mayor Tom Arceneaux and Chief Wayne Smith provided updates on Sunday afternoon.

items.title: 8 children killed in a shooting in Louisiana, police say
items.standfirst: The victims ranged in age from 1 to about 14 years old, said Shreveport, La., police spokesperson Chris Bordelon. A total of 10 people were shot.
items.content: The victims ranged in age from 1 to about 14 years old, said Shreveport, La., police spokesperson Chris Bordelon. A total of 10 people were shot.(Image credit: Gerald Herbert)

items.title: Eight children killed in Louisiana domestic violence mass shooting
items.standfirst: Eight children died in a domestic-related mass shooting Sunday in Shreveport, Louisiana. The victims' ages ranged from one to about 14 years old, said Shreveport police spokesperson Chris Bordelon.
items.content: Eight children died in a domestic-related mass shooting Sunday in Shreveport, Louisiana. The victims' ages ranged from one to about 14 years old, said Shreveport police spokesperson Chris Bordelon.

clusters.title: Bulgaria's pro-Russian former president takes strong lead
clusters.neutral_headline: Rumen Radev looks set to win Bulgarian Parliamentary election
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World, Financial Times and others

items.title: Bulgaria's pro-Russian former president takes strong lead
items.standfirst: Bulgaria’s ex-president Rumen Radev is on course to win a parliamentary election, according to exit polls, but may fall short of a governing majority. His Progressive Bulgaria coalition leads with, in a vote marked by low turnout and deep political instability, the country’s eighth election in five years. Radev warns another vote would be “a disaster,” as Bulgaria grapples with corruption, fragmentation, and its EU-Russia balancing act. Jean-Emile Jammine speaks with Renne Traicova, Managing board at the Balkan Free Media Initiative.
items.content: Bulgaria’s ex-president Rumen Radev is on course to win a parliamentary election, according to exit polls, but may fall short of a governing majority. His Progressive Bulgaria coalition leads with, in a vote marked by low turnout and deep political instability, the country’s eighth election in five years. Radev warns another vote would be “a disaster,” as Bulgaria grapples with corruption, fragmentation, and its EU-Russia balancing act. Jean-Emile Jammine speaks with Renne Traicova, Managing board at the Balkan Free Media Initiative.

items.title: Rumen Radev looks set to win Bulgarian Parliamentary election
items.standfirst: Exit polls put his Progressive Bulgaria on 37%, more than double the score of the closest runner-up, but he will likely need to form a coalition to govern.
items.content: Exit polls put his Progressive Bulgaria on 37%, more than double the score of the closest runner-up, but he will likely need to form a coalition to govern.

items.title: Pro-Russia ex-president claims victory in Bulgaria’s elections
items.standfirst: Rumen Radev’s projected majority could turn the EU member state’s parliament away from the West
items.content: Rumen Radev’s projected majority could turn the EU member state’s parliament away from the West

items.title: Party of Bulgaria’s pro-Russian former president ahead in exit polls
items.standfirst: Rumen Radev’s centre-left Progressive Bulgaria group ran on anti-corruption platform in country’s eighth election in five yearsThe party of Bulgaria’s pro-Russia former president Rumen Radev has come first in the country’s eighth parliamentary elections in five years, according to exit polls, but without securing a majority.Radev, who resigned as president in January, ran on a pledge to fight corruption after an anti-graft movement triggered a long political crisis. Continue reading...
items.content: Rumen Radev’s centre-left Progressive Bulgaria group ran on anti-corruption platform in country’s eighth election in five yearsThe party of Bulgaria’s pro-Russia former president Rumen Radev has come first in the country’s eighth parliamentary elections in five years, according to exit polls, but without securing a majority.Radev, who resigned as president in January, ran on a pledge to fight corruption after an anti-graft movement triggered a long political crisis. Continue reading...

items.title: Exit poll shows former President Radev’s party set to win Bulgaria election
items.standfirst: Radev's Progressive Bulgaria party projected to win 38.1 percent of the vote in parliamentary election.
items.content: Radev's Progressive Bulgaria party projected to win 38.1 percent of the vote in parliamentary election.

clusters.title: Humanoid robots show rapid advances as they race past humans in Beijing half marathon
clusters.neutral_headline: Watch: Runners v robots at China half marathon
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, NBC World, France24 and others

items.title: Humanoid robots show rapid advances as they race past humans in Beijing half marathon
items.standfirst: Winning robot runs faster time than Jacob Kiplimo’s world recordMore than 100 robots run in parallel tracks to avoid collisions with humansThey can already carry the shopping, cook and clean. Now they can run and win half marathons.In perhaps the most unusual spectacle ever seen at the end of the 13.1-mile (21.1km) race, robots flew over the finish line ahead of the humans for the first time in Beijing on Sunday. And there wasn’t a bead of sweat in sight. Continue reading...
items.content: Winning robot runs faster time than Jacob Kiplimo’s world recordMore than 100 robots run in parallel tracks to avoid collisions with humansThey can already carry the shopping, cook and clean. Now they can run and win half marathons.In perhaps the most unusual spectacle ever seen at the end of the 13.1-mile (21.1km) race, robots flew over the finish line ahead of the humans for the first time in Beijing on Sunday. And there wasn’t a bead of sweat in sight. Continue reading...

items.title: Humanoid robot runs a half-marathon race faster than the human world record
items.standfirst: The bipedal humanoid, developed by smartphone maker Honor, blazed through the 13-mile race in just 50 minutes and 26 seconds, surpassing the human world record for a half-marathon.
items.content: The bipedal humanoid, developed by smartphone maker Honor, blazed through the 13-mile race in just 50 minutes and 26 seconds, surpassing the human world record for a half-marathon.

items.title: Humans far behind as robot breaks record at Beijing half marathon
items.standfirst: A humanoid robot competing against flesh-and-blood runners broke the world record at a Beijing half marathon on Sunday, showcasing the rapid technological advancement achieved by Chinese makers. Humanoid robots have become a common sight in China in recent years, in the media as well as in public spaces.
items.content: A humanoid robot competing against flesh-and-blood runners broke the world record at a Beijing half marathon on Sunday, showcasing the rapid technological advancement achieved by Chinese makers. Humanoid robots have become a common sight in China in recent years, in the media as well as in public spaces.

items.title: 'New era': Humanoid robot outruns humans in Beijing half-marathon, beats world record
items.standfirst: A humanoid robot on Sunday won a half-marathon for robots in Beijing in 50 minutes 26 seconds, beating the human world record time and showcasing China's technological advances.
items.content: A humanoid robot on Sunday won a half-marathon for robots in Beijing in 50 minutes 26 seconds, beating the human world record time and showcasing China's technological advances.

items.title: Watch: Runners v robots at China half marathon

items.content: Robots competed in a half marathon race in Beijing on Sunday, with the winning machine leaving its human rivals for dust.

items.title: In a race between humans and robots, machines take the victory in a sign of advancements

items.content: BEIJING — It was not even close as a bright-red Chinese humanoid named “Lightning” lived up to its name in a half-marathon pitting humans against robots, smoking its competition on Sunday

items.title: Rat poison found in baby food jar in Austria after product recall
items.standfirst: Police say poison detected in jar of HiPP carrots and potatoes as maker says items may have been tampered withRat poison has been found in a jar of HiPP baby food, police in Austria have said, after a recall of the product from more than 1,000 Spar supermarkets in the country over safety fears.Police in Burgenland said in a statement that a sample from one of the 190g (7oz) jars of carrots and potatoes baby food reported by a customer had tested positive for rat poison. items.title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn reuses booster but upper stage misses orbit
items.standfirst: Blue Origin achieved its first New Glenn booster reuse, but the rocket’s upper stage put the payload into the wrong orbit, undercutting the launch’s overall success. [arstechnica.com]
items.content: Blue Origin achieved its first New Glenn booster reuse, but the rocket’s upper stage put the payload into the wrong orbit, undercutting the launch’s overall success. [arstechnica.com]

items.content: The launching appeared to go off without a hitch, but a problem prevented the rocket's upper stage from putting its payload into the correct orbit.
